text: The surgeon begins by inspecting the abdominal cavity, checking the anastomosis marked in blue. The distal ileum is identified, and a gap is created through the mesentery to transect the terminal ileum, separating it from the surgical specimen. An endostapler is used to transect the ileum near the anastomosis. Subsequently, the mesentery of the distal ileum is transected up to the lateral side of the cecum, detaching the mesentery from the right colon.

The surgeon begins a lateral to medial dissection through the Toldt's fascia near the colon. A partial resection of Gerota's fascia is performed to ensure clear margins near the tumor. The right kidney is visible, and the resected portion of Gerota's fascia is marked in green.

The surgeon completes the lateral mobilization of the right colon up to the hepatic flexure, continuing dissection close to the colon. The gastroepiploic ligament is then addressed to mobilize the transverse colon, separating it from the gallbladder and approaching the tumor region where it invades the liver.

The surgeon performs a medial to lateral dissection, carefully dissecting the ileocecal vessels near the superior mesenteric vein. The mesentery is opened along the vein's axis, creating an open space to separate the pancreas and duodenum from the specimen. The duodenum and pancreas are visibly free from the specimen, allowing for secure dissection and ligation of the ileocecal vessels using a clip and bipolar advanced energy device.

The surgeon completes the duodenal detachment and mobilization, nearly finishing the medial to lateral dissection. The transverse mesocolon is ligated, and a gap is created for the insertion of the endostapler. The distal margin is transected near the previous anastomosis, with the transverse colon being stapled to ensure a proper distal margin.

The surgeon performs a partial hepatectomy using bipolar advanced energy, sealing and treating the liver parenchyma. Following the hepatectomy, the tumor remains attached to the Gerota's fascia, and medial dissection is carried out to complete the hepatic flexure mobilization and right colectomy.

The video shows the partial resection of Gerota's fascia, highlighted in green. Hemostasis is achieved using monopolar energy and Surgicel is placed for additional control. The specimen is extracted through a mylar incision, and a closed suction drain is inserted near the liver. The specimen is displayed, with a pathological report indicating PT-IV PN-Ib, two positive lymph nodes out of fifty, and three margins of resection.
